title: The agentic loop: request → tool_use → tool_result → repeat

body.overview: An agentic loop is a while-loop in your application that keeps calling Claude until it stops requesting tools. Each iteration: send messages to Claude, check if the response contains tool_use blocks, execute the requested tools, append tool_result messages, and repeat. The loop exits when Claude responds with only text (end_turn) — meaning it has enough information to answer.

components.name: Your application loop
components.role: Orchestrator: sends requests, executes tools, feeds results back

components.name: Claude API
components.role: Decision engine: analyzes context, chooses tools or produces final answer

components.name: tool_use block
components.role: Claude's structured request specifying which tool and what arguments

components.name: tool_result block
components.role: Your execution output sent back as the next conversation turn

body.flow: 1. Send user message + tool definitions to Claude. 2. Claude returns response with stop_reason. 3. If stop_reason is 'tool_use': extract tool name and args, execute in your code, append tool_result message. 4. Send updated messages back to Claude. 5. Repeat from step 2. 6. If stop_reason is 'end_turn': return Claude's text to the user. The loop may run many iterations — Claude can chain multiple tool calls to complete complex tasks.
